First Status Report Of Administrator Re Administration Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-05-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Brewer for and with Susan Phelan. Christopher Cole (Counsel) also present. Zoe Littman also present.

Mr. Cole stated that Ms. Brewer would be substituting in, that he would file the Substitution of Attorney by this week, that the partition action was sent to arbitration with JAMS, that they have mediation scheduled for June 30, 2011 and that nothing was happening in the Estate case because the main assets are tied up in the partition action. In addition, Mr. Cole stated that the Market Street property was not involved in the partition action, that the property is professionally managed and the income from the property is deposited into the Trust accounts. Furhermore, Mr. Cole stated that intent is to keep the Market Street property, that the 24th Street and the Ashbury Street property are in the partition action, that the American Arbitration Association will have to make a determination as to whether the Partner waived certain rights as well as other issues and that no Attorney's fees have been paid. The Court granted the status report. A further status report with the accounting to be filed was set for September 14, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court advised all Parties that they are not to sell, transfer or encumber any of the properties.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Rob Harris.

First Status Report Of Administrator Re Administration Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-01-2010 Continued To Nov-17-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Cole for Susan Phelan.

Mr. Cole stated that the Inventory and Appraisal would be filed today, that the business Partner had not provided information, that he asked the Petitioner to allow him to play a stronger role and that all of the assets were now accounted for. In addition, Mr. Cole stated that Zoe (Minor) was away at college, and that there was no conflict with the Mother and the Minor. The Court stated that the porperty could be distributed outright to Zoe. Mr. Cole stated that there was not enough income on the property to pay for expenses, that money from the co-owned property was used to pay on the solely owned property and that Zoe's interest was not being prejudiced in any way by how things are being done. The Court expressed concern about the delay/reluctance in distribution of the estate to Zoe and stated that it may need to put someone else in place to administer the estate. The matter was continued to Novmeber 17, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing of the petition for final distribution and for the Executor to come in and explain why the estate should not be distributed to her Daughter. The Guardianship matter was also continued to November 17, 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PGN-08-291692 Guardianship of Zoe Renee Litman.

Appearances: Christopher Cole for and with Susan Phelan. Zoe Littman was also present.

The Court stated that the Estate status report was not verified, and that it was not set for hearing, and expressed concern about the assets not being marshalled in the Guardianship and no inventory and appraisal being filed in the Estate matter. Mr. Cole stated that both Parties were in agreement with how the Estate has been handled, that they would have the appraisal by the 15th of next month, that the two properties were co-owned, that the delay was in order to get the best result for Zoe, that they had one Inventory and Appraisal, that the Appraiser needed financial information on the other property, that the records were in possession of the surviving Co-owner and that they received a lot of records in March. The matter was continued to July 28, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If said documents are not filed by then, a status report is needed. The Court is not willing to close the Guardianship matter at this time.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
